political.s: Mondelez shows bipartisan political engagement with roughly balanced FEC donations ($9,500 Republican, $10,000 Democrat from $52K total raised). The company maintains a moderate political stance with lobbying focused on trade and regulatory issues affecting global snack operations.

charity.s: Mondelez demonstrates strong charitable commitment through its Foundation, which has invested over $50M in community health and wellness programs since 2012. The company donated approximately $48M in cash and in-kind donations in 2024 with 40,000 volunteer hours by employees.
charity.focus: Child nutrition, healthy lifestyles, cocoa farming communities, humanitarian relief, youth development

environment.s: Mondelez achieved all A ratings from CDP in 2026 for climate, forest management, and water stewardship. The company has achieved 60% of its 2030 emissions reduction target, 75% of recycled plastic goals, and 100% of cocoa sourced through sustainable Cocoa Life program.

labor.s: Mondelez maintains OHSAS 18001 certification for workplace safety and reported 18% reduction in Total Recordable Incidents. Child labor monitoring covers 89% of West African cocoa communities with remediation systems, though structural challenges in cocoa supply chains persist.

dei.s: Mondelez has positioned itself as pro-DEI with commitment to double Black representation in U.S. management by 2024, appointed Global D&I Officer, and engaged third-party racial equity audit. However, recent reports indicate the company removed DEI content from its 2025 website and annual report amid shifting political landscape.

animals.s: N/A - Mondelez is a snack and confectionery company. No animal testing conducted for products.

guns.s: N/A - Mondelez is a food and snacking company with no involvement in firearms manufacturing or sales.

privacy.s: Mondelez has established Ethics & Compliance programs with global data handling policies. Limited public transparency on specific privacy practices or data security standards. Company operates integrity helpline and compliance mechanisms across 90+ countries.

execPay.s: CEO Dirk Van de Put earned $24.5M in 2025, representing a 657:1 ratio versus median employee pay per AFL-CIO data. CEO compensation includes fixed income of 10%, STI bonus of 18%, and substantial equity awards with 8x salary shareholding requirements.

sec.note: Pay ratio extracted from proxy filing — verify manually for accuracy
